Q1.insight: Users repeatedly buy from the same categories due to lack of exploration incentives and poor customer experience with new categories.

Q2.insight: Users are prevented from exploring new categories due to issues like delayed deliveries, high cancellation fees, and poor customer service.
citations: delivery always delayed and if cancel charges us stupids worst experience
order is half hour late then also ther is no option to cancel order and get refund

Q3.insight: Users discover products through the app, but the process can be frustrating due to issues like incorrect orders and unhelpful customer support.
citations: i ordered momos, but i was sent pizza instead.
very bad customer service. item not received and customer service team saying we can only report this issue.

Q4.insight: Habits play a significant role in shopping behavior, as users tend to stick with what they know and avoid new categories due to past negative experiences.

Q5.insight: Users need accurate and reliable information about products, delivery times, and customer support before trying a new category.
citations: good app, but could be better. it should shortout food according to lower to higher price for each restaurant and all restaurants together too.

Q6.insight: Frustrations emerge repeatedly due to issues like delayed deliveries, high cancellation fees, and unhelpful customer support.

Q7.insight: No specific user segments are identified as more likely to experiment, but users who have had positive experiences with the app may be more open to trying new categories.

Q8.insight: Unmet needs emerge consistently across discussions, including the need for reliable delivery times, accurate order fulfillment, and helpful customer support.
